Off Calendar Apr-01-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for and with Rebecca Paul, representative of Jewish Family & Children's Services (JFCS).

Mr. Feder addressed the 3 issues that the probate examiner had in regard to fees. That in regard to fees chargeable to this trust the trustee had been ordered to distribute jewelry in the estate, that they had a difficult time ascertaining where the beneficiaries (other than Teagen) were and if they wanted to participate, they were not responsive to the notices and that the funds came from the parent trust which are the same funds as in Teagan's trust. In regards to the occasional use of additional JFCS staff, it was felt prudent to have a second person involved when handling tangible personal property where there is no clear inventory due to the significant litigation in this case, that it is sometimes necessary at meetings to have the "person who knows" as well as "the person who can make decisions" in attendance and that the charges were for less than 7 hours. In regards to attorney's fees there was a duplication of fees that had to be corrected and that adjustments were made. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Off Calendar Oct-20-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for Jewish Family & Children's Services ("JFCS"). Rebecca Paul from JFCS also present.

Mr. Feder and Ms. Paul reported that Teagan has started school at San Francisco State, that Teagan has decided to stay in San Francisco, that she is very attached to the Grandmother's belongings, that she wishes to live in the Grandmother's home/condo and live with a roommate, and that they may have no choice but to enter into a rental agreement with the roommate. Mr. Feder also explained that one of the bids was higher than the others because it includes granite counter tops and that repairs would still be in the mid to higher $50,000.00 range. The Court granted the Petition for Authority to Contract for Repairs to Real Property (subject to review of the order).

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-12-2013 Continued To Mar-26-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for Jewish Family & Childrens Services ("JFCS") (Petitioner/Trustee).

Mr. Feder addressed the issue of publication on the Petition to Sell Real Property, stated that he is not aware if Teagan would like to live in the property, that he is not aware that Teagan has been spoken to regarding the above, that the last communication with Teagan was three years ago, and that he has asked for meeting and has received no response from the Mother. The Court expressed concern about what JFCS was doing with the property, stated that the property was unrented for four years, and questioned how JFCS will get 3% if the property is sold. Mr. Feder also stated that the Trust does not call for distribution to Teagan unless she attends college, that there will be a more significant tax liability if the property is rented, that the Mother agreed to sell the property about eighteen months ago and that they determined that there was about a nine month overlap. The Court stated that publication is required, questioned when the property was transferred and pointed out that there is a discrepancy in the transfer date, and questioned how Mr. Feder came up with the $945.00 monthly amount regarding duplication. The Accounting Petition and the Petition for Authority to Sell Real Property were continued to March 26, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for some verification as to when the property was transferred, for further clarification on how fees were calculated in Teagan's Trust and how fees were calculated in the Nana Peter's Trust, and for a declaration from the Trustee as to what contact has occurred with the family.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy J. Chen.
